These dedicated spaces will offer an expanded assortment of Apple products and accessories, and feature "Target Tech Consultants" trained by Apple. This collaboration aims to enhance the customer experience and builds on Target's positive sales performance, including a 102% increase in digital sales during the holiday season.

feed.title: Hacker drains Baden woman's bank account after intercepting call, taking over iPhone
feed.summary: A Baden woman, Donna Francis, was scammed out of nearly $1,500 after a hacker allegedly intercepted her call to Apple Support, convinced her to install remote access software, and drained her bank account. This incident follows a similar phone call interception scam in Allegheny County involving COVID-19 vaccine appointments. The FBI advises extreme caution and verification when a support person requests remote access to a device.

feed.title: Target to open 'mini Apple shops' in select stores, offer more products online. Will your location get an upgrade?
feed.summary: Target is introducing "mini Apple shops" within 17 select stores across the country, expanding Apple's physical footprint and offering an enhanced shopping experience. These dedicated spaces will showcase iPhones, iPads, Apple Watches, AirPods, and accessories, with Target tech employees receiving specialized training from Apple. The initiative aims to capitalize on the popularity of Apple products among Target shoppers and builds on Target's strategy of partnering with national brands to elevate its in-store experience.

feed.title: Apple is Berkshire Hathaway's biggest equity investment, so why did Warren Buffett sell shares?
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway sold 9.81 million shares of Apple stock in Q4 2020 for nearly $1.1 billion, matching the initial purchase quantity from 2016. Despite the sale, Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway's largest equity investment, valued at around $120 billion. The sale provided funds to invest in new stakes in companies like Verizon, Marsh & McLennan, and Chevron, diversifying Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio while reducing concentration in Apple.

feed.title: Deals: Apple AirPods Pro $189, $100 off SanDisk Portable SSD
feed.summary: Amazon-owned Woot has reissued a flash deal on Apple AirPods Pro, bringing their price down to $189 (a $60 discount) for one day only. Separately, a 1TB SanDisk Extreme Portable SSD is also on sale for $149.99, saving buyers $100 off the MSRP. Various other Apple products, such as Magic Keyboards, MacBook Pros, and iMacs, are also available at discounted prices through AppleInsider and authorized resellers.

feed.title: Goldman consumer head is leaving to run Walmart fintech
feed.summary: Omer Ismail, who was instrumental in building Goldman Sachs' consumer-banking business, is departing to lead Walmart's new financial-technology startup. Ismail's move comes as Walmart seeks to expand its financial services offerings, with David Stark also joining from Goldman's team. This leadership change highlights both Goldman's ongoing adjustments in its consumer division and Walmart's strategic push into fintech.

feed.title: MicroLED, mini LED, OLED - understanding Apple's future iPhone, iPad, and Mac displays
feed.summary: This article explains the differences and applications of TFT LCD, OLED, mini-LED, and microLED display technologies, focusing on Apple's current and future use cases for its iPhone, iPad, and Mac products. It details how mini LED enhances traditional LCDs with localized dimming, while OLED offers self-illuminating pixels, and microLED represents the most radical shift with inorganic, ultra-small, self-emissive pixels. The piece highlights Apple's investment in these technologies and their potential impact on product design and user experience.

feed.title: Goldman loses 4 executives in 3 days as Walmart fintech poaches Marcus chief
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs is experiencing a significant executive exodus, with four executives departing in three days, including Marcus chief Omer Ismail and deputy David Stark who are joining Walmart's new fintech venture. Karen Seymour, Goldman's general counsel, is also leaving, and Eric Lane, co-head of asset management, is moving to Tiger Global Management. These departures highlight Walmart's serious entry into the fintech space and pose a challenge for Goldman's consumer banking division.

feed.title: Vizio Aims for Some of That Roku-like Magic with IPO
feed.summary: Vizio is pursuing an initial public offering, positioning itself as a technology company akin to Roku by leveraging its SmartCast operating system and "Platform+" business. The company reported a 20% increase in smart TV shipments and 11% revenue growth in 2020, with its SmartCast OS gathering 12.2 million active users. Vizio aims for its Platform+ segment, focused on advertising and data sales, to drive future margin growth and financial performance, similar to how Roku's platform revenues have outperformed hardware sales.

feed.title: Gatorade Debuts Wearable Patch To Measure Hydration Needs
feed.summary: Gatorade has introduced the Gx Sweat Patch and a companion Gx App, a new wearable technology designed to measure an individual's unique sweat profile for personalized hydration recommendations. The patch is used during a single workout, and its data, scanned by the app, provides tailored advice for hydration before, during, and after exercise. This new product is part of Gatorade's expanding Gx platform, though the company recently faced an intellectual property challenge regarding its Gatorlyte product.

feed.title: KT and LG Uplus Invest in SKT’s Subsidiary One Store to Unite in Building Korea’s Leading App Store
feed.summary: KT and LG Uplus have invested a total of KRW 26 billion in SKT's subsidiary, One Store, acquiring a 3.8% stake to strengthen Korea's ICT ecosystem and enhance local app store competitiveness. This investment aims to deepen collaboration among the three mobile operators (SKT, KT, LG Uplus) and further increase One Store's market presence by expanding content offerings and user base. One Store, established in 2016, achieved its first net income surplus last year and has seen consistent gross merchandise value growth, proactively implementing policies to support content providers.

feed.title: Arizona House passes bill that threatens Apple and Google's app stores
feed.summary: The Arizona House of Representatives passed a bill (HB 2005) that would compel app stores, specifically targeting Apple and Google, to allow app makers to use their own payment processing software to bypass the 15-30% fees charged. The legislation, which passed 31-29, is a setback for Apple and Google but a victory for groups like the Coalition for App Fairness. While the bill still requires Senate approval and the governor's signature, it highlights ongoing legislative efforts to regulate app store policies following criticism of their alleged anticompetitive practices.

feed.title: KT and LG U+ buy into OneStore, the Google Play killer
feed.summary: South Korean mobile carriers KT and LG U+ have acquired stakes in One Store, a local app market, joining SK Telecom to challenge the dominance of Google Play and Apple App Store. This strategic investment aims to bolster the domestic IT ecosystem and comes as foreign app markets face increasing criticism over their in-app payment policies. One Store saw a significant increase in transaction volume and turned a profit for the first time in 2020, benefiting from the controversy surrounding Google's 30 percent commission policy.

feed.title: Brent Potts joined Airbnb, Inc. as Vice President of Community Support
feed.summary: Brent Potts has joined Airbnb, Inc. as Vice President of Community Support, bringing extensive experience from Apple and Hewlett-Packard. His role will focus on enhancing support for Airbnb's global community of hosts and guests, aiming to further elevate the company's customer service standards. Potts previously led AppleCare divisions, streamlining customer support journeys for billions of customers.

feed.title: The Parent of All Parents: Here's What Berkshire Hathaway Actually Does
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway is a holding company led by Warren Buffett that owns about 70 private companies and minority stakes in 41 public stocks across various sectors. While it doesn't engage in manufacturing, it controls company shares and investment interests. Buffett is the top shareholder, and notable holdings include significant stakes in Apple and Coca-Cola, with a clear disinterest in investing in Tesla.

feed.title: Four Investments of Nancy Pelosi and Her Husband Reveal Much
feed.summary: The article discusses four significant investments made by House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and her husband, Paul Pelosi, on December 22. These investments include shares in AllianceBernstein (AB) and call options in Apple (AAPL), Tesla (TSLA), and Walt Disney Co (DIS), totaling between $1,751,003 and $3,500,000. Experts weigh in on the implications of these bullish trades, noting that while the Pelosis are wealthy, their investment choices align with market opportunities, although the full scope and reasoning behind the trades are not publicly known.

feed.title: Tech-heavy Nasdaq hits correction with 10.5% fall from peak
feed.summary: The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite has entered correction territory, falling 10.5% from its February peak, as technology shares experienced a significant sell-off. This downturn was driven by concerns over rising bond yields and inflation fears stemming from a new $1.9 trillion COVID-19 relief bill. While financial, restaurant, and travel stocks rose on reopening hopes, they couldn't offset the weight of declining big tech shares like Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla.

feed.title: Autodesk Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) shares fell 5.34% on Monday, closing at $253.10, despite a mixed trading session for the broader market. The stock underperformed against key competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL), Adobe Inc. (ADBE), and Synopsys Inc. (SNPS), all of which also experienced declines. Trading volume for Autodesk surpassed its 50-day average.

feed.title: CDW Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Shares of CDW Corp. (CDW) rose 0.43% to $155.14 on Monday, outperforming the S&P 500 Index despite a mixed trading session. The stock closed below its 52-week high but showed a mixed performance against competitors like Apple Inc. (AAPL) and HP Inc. (HPQ). Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

feed.title: Tesla loses a third of its value for the third time in a year
feed.summary: Tesla Inc.'s stock experienced its third significant drop in a year, losing a third of its value from its January record high, primarily due to investor concerns about rising interest rates affecting high-valuation stocks. The selloff wiped off nearly $300 billion from its market capitalization, moving it behind Facebook. This decline is more pronounced than other major tech companies, and its shares have steadily fallen since announcing a bitcoin purchase, even as bitcoin’s price climbed.

feed.title: The Sonos Roam is a small, portable smart speaker for $169
feed.summary: Sonos has unveiled its smallest and most portable smart speaker to date, the Sonos Roam, priced at $169. This lightweight device, weighing under a pound, is designed for on-the-go use and features an IP67 rating for water and dust resistance, as well as Qi wireless charging compatibility. It boasts enhanced sound quality with Automatic TruePlay Tuning, connects via Wi-Fi and Bluetooth, and supports over 100 streaming services and multiple voice assistants.

feed.title: WWE® SuperCard Celebrates 25 Years of “Stone Cold” Steve Austin with New Content Available Now
feed.summary: 2K has announced new content for WWE SuperCard to celebrate the 25th anniversary of "Stone Cold" Steve Austin's WWE debut. Throughout March, players can enjoy new items and events, including a "Stone Cold" Steve Austin cardback, his iconic jean shorts, and a "Stone Cold" Fusion event starting March 9th to create Special Edition cards.

feed.title: The 10 most innovative companies in consumer electronics
feed.summary: This article highlights the top 10 most innovative companies in consumer electronics, showcasing how they adapted and thrived during the pandemic by releasing useful new products. It features companies like Corning, Apple, and Samsung alongside newer players such as Raspberry Pi, Tempo, and Aira. The piece details Corning's advancements in Gorilla Glass and Ceramic Shield, emphasizing their impact on device durability.

feed.title: Video Games & Esports: Building on 2020’s Rapid Growth
feed.summary: The video game industry experienced significant growth in 2020, driven by the pandemic, new console releases, and the rise of social gaming, with total revenue reaching $175 billion. This article explores key trends poised to continue this expansion, including the increasing impact of digital distribution, the monetization strategies of free-to-play games, the evolution of social gaming into metaverse-like experiences, and the growing prominence of mobile gaming. It also highlights the role of game engines in facilitating development for smaller studios and concludes that the industry is set for continued strong performance and investor opportunities.

feed.title: Sonos Announces the AirPlay 2-Compatible Roam Portable Speaker
feed.summary: Sonos has unveiled the Roam, a new portable, battery-powered speaker that supports AirPlay 2 and is designed for both indoor and outdoor use. Priced at $169, the speaker features IP67 water resistance, 10 hours of playback, and automatic switching between WiFi and Bluetooth. It also incorporates Sonos' Trueplay technology for adaptive audio and supports Sound Swap, Amazon Alexa, and Google Assistant.

feed.title: Patent Filings Roundup: Fintiv Dooms CBMs; Canadian Process Ends IPR; New and Old NPE Campaigns Filed
feed.summary: This article summarizes recent patent filings, noting a decrease in PTAB filings compared to district court patent litigation. It highlights key rulings, including the termination of CBM proceedings against Boom! Payments due to a Federal Circuit 101 ruling and the PTAB's denial of a petition based on Canadian service-of-process precedent. The piece also details new and continuing patent enforcement campaigns by various entities, including a significant increase in DynaIP subsidiary litigation.

feed.title: Why a Growth Stock Fund Is Betting on Align Technology, DocuSign, and Apple
feed.summary: The Lord Abbett Growth Leaders fund, managing $8.1 billion, differentiates itself by integrating technical analysis into its investment process, a less common approach for large mutual funds. Thomas O'Halloran, one of the fund's managers, oversees this strategy, which includes investments in companies like Align Technology, DocuSign, and Apple. The article highlights this unique methodology in growth stock investing.

feed.title: Industrial ETFs Like 'DUSL' Riding a Wave of Market Momentum
feed.summary: The article discusses the recent market shift towards industrial stocks, citing the Direxion Daily Industrials Bull 3X Shares (DUSL) as a potential investment for leveraged gains. It highlights how industrials are performing well as the economy strengthens, attracting capital away from technology stocks. The SPDR Industrial ETF (XLI) has reached a new high, contrasting with the SPDR Technology ETF (XLK)'s decline, indicating a move towards value stocks benefiting from economic growth and higher interest rates.

feed.title: Apple starts iPhone 12 assembly in India
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has begun assembling its iPhone 12 in India, marking a significant ramp-up of its manufacturing operations in the country. This move, undertaken with contract manufacturer Foxconn, aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify production away from China due to ongoing trade tensions and India's efforts to boost its smartphone manufacturing sector. India's technology minister, Ravi Shankar Prasad, welcomed the development, highlighting its potential to create numerous jobs.

feed.title: Netflix password sharing crackdown underway with new test and warning
feed.summary: Netflix is implementing a new test to curb password sharing, requesting users to verify account ownership to ensure they are authorized to use the service. This move comes as the streaming market has become increasingly competitive, with password sharing costing providers billions in lost revenue, an amount expected to rise to $12.5 billion by 2024. Users who do not verify their account will be asked to create a new one, as Netflix aims to enforce its terms of service that limit sharing to individuals within the same household.

feed.title: Target, The 'Apple Experience' And Training
feed.summary: This article discusses Target's strategy for staffing its new in-store Apple kiosks with specially trained employees, emphasizing the importance of knowledgeable staff for customer satisfaction and sales. It highlights how co-branding extends to employee training, crucial for retail partnerships and reducing employee turnover, while warning against exclusive training that could undermine collaborative culture.

feed.title: Richest people in the world: Warren Buffett is now worth $100 billion
feed.summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has seen his net worth climb to $100 billion, making him the sixth richest person globally. His company's Class A shares are up nearly 15% this year, and his net worth has increased by approximately $13 billion. Buffett, at 90, has stated he has no plans to retire, and Berkshire Hathaway recently added Verizon, Chevron, Marsh & McLennan, and EW Scripps to its portfolio while selling off stakes in JPMorgan Chase and Pfizer.

feed.title: Apple signs order for 'The Jet' about iconic 90s Pepsi Points Harrier promo
feed.summary: Apple TV+ has ordered a docuseries titled "The Jet" which will delve into the notorious 1996 Pepsi Points Harrier Jet promotion. The series will explore the events surrounding John Leonard's lawsuit against PepsiCo after he attempted to redeem 7 million Pepsi Points for a Harrier Jump Jet. Directed by James Lee Hernandes and Brian Lazarte, the docuseries promises a deep dive into 90s pop culture and the legal battle that ensued.

feed.title: The New Possible Podcast: Vortic Watch Co: Not Your Grandpa’s Pocket Watch
feed.summary: This article introduces an episode of The New Possible podcast featuring Vortic Watch Co., a company that repurposes WWII-era American pocket watches into modern wristwatches. Founders RT Custer and Tyler Wolfe discuss their business model, which involves restoring old pocket watch mechanisms and manufacturing new cases and components using Autodesk Fusion 360, focusing on transparent and U.S.-based production. The podcast highlights their journey, including pivoting ideas, crowdfunding success, and bringing manufacturing in-house.

feed.title: Semiconductors as a shield for Taiwan?
feed.summary: Taiwan's independence is significantly bolstered by its semiconductor industry, particularly Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). TSMC's critical role in the global supply chain for microprocessors provides Taiwan with a strategic advantage, deterring China from invasion due to potential economic repercussions and compelling the United States to protect its access to these vital technologies. The company's capabilities and importance make a military conflict over Taiwan highly costly for both China and the US, thus acting as a powerful "silicon shield."
feed.source: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site
feed.source_domain: The Strategist | ASPI's analysis and commentary site

feed.title: Qualcomm Completes Acquisition of NUVIA
feed.summary: Qualcomm Incorporated announced the completion of its acquisition of NUVIA for $1.4 billion. This acquisition aims to enhance Qualcomm's CPU roadmap, integrating next-generation CPUs across various products including smartphones, laptops, and automotive systems. The first Qualcomm Snapdragon platforms featuring these new internally designed CPUs are expected to sample in the second half of 2022.

feed.title: Nokia to cut up to 10,000 jobs worldwide
feed.summary: Nokia plans to cut between 5,000 and 10,000 jobs globally over the next two years to reduce costs by €600m (£518m). The telecoms giant aims to invest in 5G, cloud computing, and digital infrastructure research to improve its performance against rivals. Job cuts are expected across various regions, with about 96 roles in the UK and 300 in Finland under threat.

feed.title: Apple v. Iancu: Oral Argument on Motion to Dismiss and Plaintiff Summary Judgment Motion, PTAB Litigation Blog
feed.summary: This article discusses the oral argument in the case of Apple v. Iancu, focusing on motions to dismiss and for summary judgment. The lawsuit, filed by major tech companies, challenges the PTAB's "Fintiv" rule, which allows for discretionary denial of patent institution petitions when parallel litigation exists. The plaintiffs argue the rule exceeds statutory authority, is arbitrary and capricious, and was adopted without proper rulemaking procedures.

feed.title: Laptop Bench Test: Dell Latitude 5410 – An upgrade in mediocrity?
feed.summary: This article reviews the Dell Latitude 5410 laptop, evaluating if it offers significant upgrades over its predecessor or remains a mediocre device. The review details specifications, performance benchmarks (Cinebench, GeekBench), and its suitability for corporate use, concluding it's a solid "fleet laptop" for business tasks rather than a high-performance personal device.

feed.title: At $2.08 Trillion, Apple Is Bigger Than These Things
feed.summary: As of March 15, 2021, Apple Inc. (AAPL) boasts a market capitalization of $2.08 trillion, making it the world's largest public company. This staggering figure surpasses the GDP of most countries, the total market value of many global stock exchanges, the U.S. budget deficit, and the inflation-adjusted costs of multiple major U.S. wars. It also exceeds the combined net worth of the world's 18 richest people.

feed.title: Apple’s $4.7 billion Green Bond spend is helping to create 1.2 gigawatts of clean power
feed.summary: Apple has invested $4.7 billion from its Green Bonds to fund 17 projects that will generate 1.2 gigawatts of clean energy globally and avoid 921,000 metric tons of carbon emissions annually. These projects include solar farms in Nevada and Virginia, a wind farm in Illinois, and large onshore wind turbines in Denmark. Apple aims to be carbon neutral across its entire business, manufacturing supply chain, and product life cycle by 2030, building on its already carbon-neutral global corporate operations.

feed.title: QYLG Stock Price | Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call & Growth ETF
feed.summary: This article provides comprehensive information about the Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call & Growth ETF (QYLG), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key financial metrics like dividend yield, expense ratio, and market capitalization. It also features technical analysis indicators and a list of its top holdings, offering a detailed overview for potential investors.

feed.title: “Think twice about whose money you take”: 21 tips for aspiring unicorns
feed.summary: Lior Handelsman, co-founder of SolarEdge and now with Grove Ventures, shares 21 essential tips for aspiring entrepreneurs, drawn from his experiences building SolarEdge into a billion-dollar company and investing in startups. His advice covers critical aspects like aiming big, securing funding, managing valuations, team dynamics, and the psychological demands of entrepreneurship.

feed.title: Clarivate celebrates BD’s culture of innovation
feed.summary: Clarivate has named BD to its Top 100 Global Innovators 2021 list for the sixth time, recognizing the company's significant investment in innovation and its commitment to protecting intellectual property. BD invests over a billion dollars annually in innovation, contributing to advancements in medical technology, and holds approximately 27,000 active, issued patents worldwide. The recognition highlights BD's ongoing efforts to advance global health through pioneering solutions and a robust intellectual property portfolio.

feed.title: Coral South FLNG Project, Rovuma Basin, Mozambique
feed.summary: The Coral South FLNG Project involves the development of the Coral Sul floating liquefied natural gas (FLNG) facility in the Coral South field, located in Area 4 of the Rovuma Basin off Mozambique. Operated by Eni, the project aims to monetize gas reserves and is expected to start production in 2022. The article details the project's financing, contractors, and the technical specifications of the FLNG unit.

feed.title: How Texas is luring big businesses and billionaires away from California
feed.summary: Texas is experiencing a significant economic boom, attracting numerous companies and wealthy individuals from California, including major tech firms like Oracle, Tesla, and Apple. This migration is fueled by factors prompting a "tremendous increase" in relocations and expansions, making Texas a burgeoning business hub. The article explores the reasons behind this trend and its potential implications for the state's future.

feed.title: 3 Mid-Cap Global Battery Manufacturer Stocks
feed.summary: This article examines three mid-cap global battery manufacturers: Varta AG (Germany), EnerSys (USA), and Simplo Technology (Taiwan), as potential investment opportunities given the growing demand for lithium-ion batteries. It details each company's business model, revenue streams, and market position, highlighting both their strengths and potential risks. The piece concludes by suggesting that foreign battery stocks can offer diversification for investors looking beyond domestic options.

feed.title: Scammers trick Android users into installing fake, malware-laden Clubhouse app
feed.summary: Scammers are exploiting the demand for the audio-based social app Clubhouse among Android users by distributing a fake, malware-laden version of the app. This fraudulent Android app, discovered by ESET researchers, contains a Trojan virus called "BlackRock" that can steal login information from over 458 online services, including financial apps, social media, and cryptocurrency exchanges, and can also intercept text messages, bypassing two-factor authentication. Experts warn that the absence of an official Android version of Clubhouse creates a significant vulnerability that cybercriminals are eager to exploit, making an official Android app the ultimate solution.

feed.title: Intel to Spend $20 Billion on 2 New Chip Factories in Arizona. (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Intel's new CEO, Patrick Gelsinger, announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip factories in Arizona. This move signifies Intel's commitment to doubling down on chip manufacturing in the U.S. and Europe, aiming to become a major manufacturer for other companies, addressing concerns about component shortages and reliance on Asian factories. The initiative also seeks to regain Intel's footing in the competitive chip production market, where it had previously fallen behind competitors like TSMC and Samsung.

feed.title: Lucid Motors Deepens Expertise with Executive Team Additions and Promotions
feed.summary: Lucid Motors has strengthened its executive team with significant additions and promotions as it prepares for customer deliveries of its Lucid Air EV in the second half of 2021. The company appointed Michael Bell as Senior VP of Digital, Achim Pantfoerder as VP of Program Management, and Nicolas Minbiole as VP of Global Quality. Additionally, Eric Bach was promoted to Senior VP – Product and Chief Engineer, and Derek Jenkins to Senior VP – Design and Brand, demonstrating Lucid's commitment to quality and innovation.

feed.title: Lumentum raises Coherent buyout offer to $7 bln
feed.summary: Optical fiber firm Lumentum Holdings Inc (LITE) has increased its buyout offer for Coherent Inc (COHR) to approximately $7 billion, intensifying a three-way bidding war for the laser manufacturer. This new offer surpasses a previous bid from II-VI Inc (IIVI) and involves a combination of cash and Lumentum shares for Coherent shareholders. The battle for Coherent began after Lumentum initially offered $5.7 billion, drawing rival bids from MKS Instruments and II-VI.

feed.title: SEC is investigating Japanese investment giant SoftBank for market manipulation
feed.summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is investigating SoftBank Group Corp. for alleged market manipulation. The investigation reportedly concerns SoftBank's role in stoking a tech stock rally in August through massive call option purchases, which generated a reported $4 billion profit. While the exact grounds for the investigation are not fully disclosed, it is tied to allegations of stock manipulation.

feed.title: Intel to spend $20 billion on U.S. chip plants as CEO challenges Asia dominance
feed.summary: Intel announced plans to invest $20 billion in two new chip manufacturing plants in Arizona, aiming to expand its advanced chip production and open its factories to outside customers. This move by CEO Pat Gelsinger directly challenges the dominance of TSMC and Samsung in the advanced chip market and seeks to shift the technological balance of power back to the U.S. and Europe. The company expects $72 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings per share of $4.55 for 2021, and the new strategy is intended to restore Intel's reputation and create 3,000 permanent jobs.

feed.title: In $20bn push, Intel hopes to make US chip-making great again
feed.summary: Intel announced a significant expansion of its advanced chip manufacturing capacity with a $20bn investment to build two factories in Arizona, aiming to regain its market leadership and challenge rivals TSMC and Samsung. CEO Pat Gelsinger's strategy includes opening Intel's plants to outside customers in a "foundry" business model and focusing on cutting-edge computing chip manufacturing. This move is also a response to US and European concerns about the concentration of chipmaking in Asia and seeks to boost domestic production.

feed.title: Newfound Popularity of Thematic ETFs
feed.summary: Thematic ETFs, which invest in baskets of stocks benefiting from emerging trends, technology, or evolving economic landscapes, have seen a significant surge in popularity since October 2020, with AUM growing by 132%. Despite this growth, the article highlights potential risks associated with these ETFs, including the reversal of investor enthusiasm impacting fund flow and performance, the concentrated holdings of individual stocks within the ETFs, and the potential for misleading diversification that may dilute thematic exposure and justify higher fees. Investors are advised against chasing "hot" ETFs without understanding their underlying holdings and potential risks.

feed.title: Stryker, Zimmer make data and analytics a top priority as robotic procedures ramp up
feed.summary: Stryker and Zimmer Biomet are highly prioritizing data and analytics to enhance their robotic surgical procedures and improve patient outcomes. Stryker's Mako system uses data from past procedures to inform implant positioning, while Zimmer Biomet partners with Apple to collect post-operative patient data via the mymobility app and Apple Watch. Both companies are also investing in smart implants and predictive analytics to further refine surgical approaches and patient care.

feed.title: Toyota VS Apple: The Age of EV Battles Unlatches the Door to Smart Cars
feed.summary: The electric vehicle (EV) market is seeing an influx of traditional automakers and tech giants like Apple and Baidu, creating a new battleground for smart cars. Toyota, the world's leading automaker, is positioning itself with AI-based software development and strategic partnerships to compete against these tech companies, focusing on a "making friends" approach similar to Google's Android strategy. While Apple boasts significant financial and brand power, Toyota believes its collaborative ecosystem for automotive technology gives it an edge in the complex and responsibility-heavy auto industry.

feed.title: Invesco unveils Nasdaq ‘next generation’ ETF
feed.summary: Invesco has launched two new ETFs, the Invesco NASDAQ Next Generation 100 UCITS ETF (EQJS) and the Invesco Nasdaq-100 Swap UCITS ETF (EQQS), expanding its tech ETF range. EQJS tracks the Nasdaq Next Generation 100 index, mirroring a US-listed strategy, while EQQS provides synthetic exposure to the Nasdaq 100, designed to mitigate withholding tax on US equity dividends. These new offerings are listed on the LSE with competitive total expense ratios.

feed.title: The China challenge: Why Intel is too strategic to fail
feed.summary: This article analyzes Intel's strategic shift to IDM 2.0 under CEO Pat Gelsinger, focusing on the company's plan to leverage internal manufacturing, external foundries, and a new Intel Foundry Services division. It examines the market changes impacting Intel, the critical threat from China's semiconductor ambitions, and the need for significant partnerships, including with IBM and the U.S. government, for Intel to regain its leadership and combat the China challenge. The authors argue that despite positive steps, Intel needs even more substantial changes, possibly a joint venture with a volume leader like Samsung, to succeed.

feed.title: Apple Encourages Staff to Get Vaccinated, Offers Paid Time Off
feed.summary: Apple is encouraging its employees to get vaccinated against Covid-19 by offering paid time off for vaccine appointments and paid sick leave for any side effects experienced. However, the company is not providing vaccination shots itself and does not have direct access to vaccines for its staff. This initiative aims to support employee health and safety while acknowledging vaccine availability is outside their direct control.

feed.title: Arm’s v9 Architecture Explains Why Nvidia Needs To Buy It
feed.summary: This article explores why Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Holdings, initially questioned, makes strategic sense following Arm's v9 architecture announcement. The v9 architecture's focus on integrating vector math, matrix math, and digital signal processing into CPUs, alongside new security features and a "Realms" memory partitioning system, suggests a future where specialized, highly integrated compute on-device is paramount. Nvidia's move to acquire Arm is positioned as a bet on this evolving compute landscape, where a single, malleable architecture can support a vast array of devices from IoT to datacenters, potentially limiting the expansion of hybrid CPU-GPU models for certain workloads.
